title: Ceiling Leak
desc: Water drips in the UnderWorld. It shouldn't. It does.

steps.text:
Water shouldn't drip in the UnderWorld. But it does, here — fat black drops that sizzle when they hit stone. A maintenance crew was sent to patch the crack three days ago. They haven't come back.

The leak has gotten worse. It's pooling now, spreading across the floor in a shape that looks almost deliberate. The foreman at the tunnel entrance hands you a bucket of fire-tar and a trowel. He won't look at the ceiling.

"Patch it. Come back. Don't listen to the dripping. It's not a pattern. It's not saying anything."

options.label: Take the tar and head in
Ask about the crew that didn't come back

steps.text:
The crack runs along the ceiling like a vein. The water seeping through it is warm — warmer than the lava runoff, warmer than it has any right to be. You slather fire-tar into the gap, and for a moment the dripping stops.

Then the crack shifts. Not widens — shifts. Like something on the other side adjusted its weight.

You finish the patch in record time. The fire-tar holds. The dripping stops. The pool on the floor begins to evaporate, steam curling upward in thin wisps.

The foreman nods when you return. He still won't look at the ceiling.

"Good. Now forget about it. Everyone does."

"They came back," the foreman says. "They just won't talk about it."

He lowers his voice. "They were up there for six hours. Came back soaking wet — not from the leak, from sweat. Wouldn't say what they saw. Wouldn't say why the patch took six hours. Just... quiet. Went home. Filed a report that said 'crack sealed, no anomalies.'"

He hands you the tar. "No anomalies. That's what you'll write too."

You go up. The crack is exactly where they said — a fissure in the ceiling, water seeping through. You patch it. It's straightforward. Twenty minutes.

But as you're leaving, you notice the previous patch — the crew's work. It's not over the crack. It's three feet to the left, over solid stone. They patched nothing. Six hours to patch nothing.

What were they doing for six hours?

You write 'crack sealed, no anomalies' and don't think about it.
